mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market cooling and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the threshold crossing, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction from high prices, forcing corporate margin compression and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields positive forward returns in >58% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which boosts short-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following positive INDPRO revisions, or signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 3.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, triggering a repricing of growth assets and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >62% of periods after the threshold is crossed, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear regimes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative direction in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and housing starts signals synchronized broad-based economic demand, which improves corporate profit expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the industrial production index below its 2017 baseline of 100.0 indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods following the threshold breach, or the signal shows no statistical edge in backtested walk-forward periods.
